failedAssertions.reason: Auth detection correctly uses `cursor-agent status`. /health shows 'auth': 'not_authenticated' when cursor-agent is not logged in. Cannot verify authenticated case without logged-in cursor-agent. cli-config.json only contains user settings (permissions, editor, model preferences) - no access tokens.

Architectural limitation: cursor-agent stores tokens internally, not in ~/.cursor/ files. cli-config.json only has user settings, NOT access tokens. This cannot be resolved without changing cursor-agent behavior.

blockedAssertions.blockedBy: Cannot test quota exceeded without triggering real API usage limits. Would require actual Cursor API quota consumption.

Cannot test 401 for missing auth because: (1) model validation happens before auth check (correct behavior per VAL-ERROR-003 fix), (2) without authenticated cursor-agent, cannot determine which models are valid. Requires authenticated cursor-agent to establish valid model list.

verifiedAssertions: VAL-PROXY-001: Health endpoint returns 200 with status ok
VAL-PROXY-002: Models endpoint returns 200 with empty list (expected without auth)
VAL-ERROR-001: Invalid JSON returns 400 with proper error
VAL-ERROR-003: Unknown model returns 400 with model_not_found

notes: Re-run verification. cursor-agent is not logged in (requires interactive login). Previously-passed assertions verified where possible without auth. Same 4 assertions remain failed/blocked due to architectural limitations and auth requirements.
